title: agoseris purpurea
intro: Agoseris purpurea, also known as purple top or purple rosinweed, is a perennial plant in the sunflower family (Asteraceae) native to western North America. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can be used to treat a range of health issues, including digestive problems, fever, and skin conditions. The medicinal properties of Agoseris purpurea are attributed to its ability to stimulate digestion, reduce inflammation, and promote wound healing. Agoseris purpurea can be used in herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, and is also used topically to apply its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties directly to affected areas.
